title: test rootless_storage_path from storage.conf
body:
Add test for PR https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/529
test rootless_storage_path from strorage.conf. If user configured rootless_storage_path in storage.conf, podman info should suggest the change.

can be merged after #5929

Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body: Testing for https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/529
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #5258) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@QiWang19 Whats the scoop on this.  Once this passes tests we can merge storage PR.

```
configPath := "/etc/containers/storage.conf"
desPath := filepath.Join(podmanTest.TempDir, "storage.conf")
cmd := exec.Command("sudo", "cp", configPath, desPath)
```
the `cp` failed. I want to try the storage.conf under $HOME

Remove the tmpdir stuff.  That must be the issue.

cp /etc/containers/storage.conf /etc/containers/storage.conf.test
And then copy it back.

> Remove the tmpdir stuff. That must be the issue.
> 
> cp /etc/containers/storage.conf /etc/containers/storage.conf.test
> And then copy it back.

fails again, cp returns the warning 
```
We trust you have received the usual lecture from the local System
[+0440s] Administrator. It usually boils down to these three things:
[+0440s] 
[+0440s]     #1) Respect the privacy of others.
[+0440s]     #2) Think before you type.
[+0440s]     #3) With great power comes great responsibility.
[+0440s] 
[+0440s] sudo: no tty present and no askpass program specified
```
@edsantiago Any way for us to run sudo from the test platform?

I doubt it, but in any case I have grave reservations about that test. It is a really, really bad idea to modify system files as part of a test, and if it has to be done it should be with lots of safeguards. I can think of many better ways to implement this test but won't have time to get into it today; I've set a tickler for myself for Monday morning.
We could bind mount over it, or maybe we just add an environment variable into libpod and test with that.
tests pass. can we merge https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/529? @edsantiago @rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat

@rhatdan thanks, that makes sense; but it still should be possible to test this by creating a dummy `$HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf`, no? Go initialization is weird, but I would hope that config file parsing happens before accessing storage, so this could all be tested rootless?
https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/529 has merged. Do I need to keep this PR?

I wish there was a way to test this.  @edsantiago Any ideas.  I think the best thing would be to add an environment variable to containers/storage to specify a different path for /etc/containers/storage.conf, then we could test this out. in Podman.  @QiWang19 Could you work on a PR to support this in container/storage.
@rhatdan did my comment above not make sense? About a dummy `storage.conf` under HOME?

> @rhatdan did my comment above not make sense? About a dummy `storage.conf` under HOME?

What is 'dummy storage.conf'?
copy the $HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf to another path under $HOME as dummy storage.conf , and in the test need an environment variable to specify dummy storage.conf?

I'm sorry, I guess I didn't explain myself correctly. I was proposing something like this:
```
$ rm -rf ~/.config/containers
$ mkdir ~/.config/containers
$ printf "[storage]\nrootless_storage_path=XXXXXXXXXX\n" > ~/.config/containers/storage.conf
$ run podman info, see if it took effect
```
My assumption here is that for purposes of this test, `/etc` and `$HOME` are indistinguishable, because podman initializes config settings early. That is: you do not actually need to change anything in `/etc`. This assumption may be incorrect.
Sure, I guess if this works, then we could merge this PR along with a vendoring of github.com/containers/storage.

tests error 
```
time="2020-02-27T16:26:22-05:00" level=error msg="'overlay' is not supported over extfs at \"/home/some14791dude/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay\""
[+0883s]       /home/some14791dude/.local/share/containers/storage
```

should the 
```
[storage]
driver="overlay"
[storage.options]
mount_program="/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
```
be added to the dummy storage.conf?
Why not just `driver="vfs"`? It really shouldn't matter for purposes of this test

> Why not just `driver="vfs"`? It really shouldn't matter for purposes of this test

Doesn't work locally. I can try in the test.

I'm vendoring a new branch https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/542. 
The `graphroot` is empty in the dummy storage.conf, to use the `rootless_storage_path`, the variable assignment should be moved out of the `if storageOpts.GraphRoot == ""{} else {}` block. And there is a path matching issue.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #5397) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@rhatdan @edsantiago PTAL. Will get this in for test storage.config `rootless_storage_path` storage.config?
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #5507) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
I will rework the tests.
@edsantiago @rhatdan PTAL.

/approve
/lgtm
